file_name: PACIRA PHARMACEUTICALS, INC. - A_R STRATEGIC LICENSING, DISTRIBUTION AND MARKETING AGREEMENT .txt

perturbation.original_text: 1. Definitions 1.1 As used in this Agreement, the following words and expressions have the following meanings: "Affiliate" With respect to any Party to this Agreement shall mean any company, corporation, firm, individual or other entity which Controls, is Controlled by or is under common Control with such Party to this Agreement for only so long as such Control exists;
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Definitions 1.1 As used in this Agreement, the following words and expressions have the following meanings: "Affiliate" With respect to any Party to this Agreement shall mean any company, corporation, firm, individual or other entity that has a business relationship with, is under consideration for a business relationship with, or has previously had a business relationship with such Party to this Agreement, regardless of Control;
perturbation.explanation: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction by changing the definition of "Affiliate" to include entities with any form of past, present or future business relationship, regardless of control. This contradicts the original, more restrictive definition tied to 'Control'. This in-text contradiction directly impacts sections of the contract that rely on the original definition of 'Affiliate', such as royalty calculations and liability clauses, creating uncertainty about which entities are subject to those clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.4 Royalties. As further consideration for the license and grant of Distribution Rights and other rights under this Agreement, EKR shall pay to PPI a royalty ("Royalty") equal to (a) $[**] for each [**] mg Vial of Product sold during the Term and $[**] for each [**] mg Vial of Product sold during the Term (the "Minimum Royalty") plus (b) an additional [**]% of any post Effective Date incremental price increase implemented by EKR over the Current Base Price of $[**] for the [**] mg Vial and $[**] for the [**] mg Vial (the "Additional Royalty"); provided, however, that Additional Royalty shall not be payable to the extent that the sum of (i) the Minimum Royalty and Additional Royalty payable hereunder and (ii) the Supply Price (as defined in the Supply Agreement) shall at any time during the Term exceed [**] percent ([**]%) of the net average selling price of the Product (the "Royalty Cap"); provided, however, that the Royalty Cap shall be [**] percent ([**]%) of the net average selling price of the Product during certain periods as described in Section 6.3(e) above. EKR shall be entitled to offset certain amounts from Royalties payable hereunder as set forth in Section 6.3(b) above. Royalties on other presentations and dosages which hereafter receive Marketing Authorization in any country of the Territory shall be negotiated in good faith by the parties in a manner consistent with the Royalty currently being paid by EKR as of the date of the receipt of Marketing Authorization for such new presentations and dosages.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of "Affiliate" to include entities with any past, present, or future business relationship, regardless of control. This directly contradicts the royalty clause (6.4) because royalties are based on the sales of 'Product' made by EKR. If 'Affiliate' now includes any company that *used* to have a business relationship with EKR, it creates significant ambiguity. The royalty clause assumes a clear definition tied to control, making it hard to apply if the definition of Affiliate has been radically changed. This inconsistency in defining a key term directly affects the applicability and enforceability of the royalty obligations, hence a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 Transfer of Transferred NDA. Effective as of the Agreement Date, PPI hereby sells, transfers, conveys and assigns to EKR all right, title and interest in and to [**] (the "Transferred NDA"). Each Party shall, within five (5) business days after the Agreement Date, file with the FDA a notice letter, substantially in the form attached as Schedule XI(A) or Schedule XI(B) (as applicable), regarding the transfer to EKR of the Transferred NDA. PPI represents, warrants and covenants that: (i) prior to the Agreement Date, it has provided EKR with complete, up- to-date copies of the Transferred NDA and all material correspondence with Regulatory Authorities in the -15- Territory in connection with the Transferred NDA (including, but not limited to, any periodic and annual report submissions, and all adverse event reports and data) and (ii) on the Agreement Date, EKR shall receive sole ownership of, and good and valid title to, the Transferred NDA, free and clear of any liens and encumbrances. For the avoidance of doubt, nothing in this Agreement regarding the appointment of EKR as PPI's distributor of the Products shall be construed to diminish any rights of EKR as holder of the Transferred NDA. Upon termination of this Agreement for any reason except by EKR pursuant to Section 16.1(a), EKR shall promptly transfer the Transferred NDA and related regulatory documentation to PPI in accordance with Section 17.1(e).
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 Transfer of Transferred NDA. Effective as of the Agreement Date, PPI hereby grants EKR a perpetual, non-exclusive license to use [**] (the "Transferred NDA"). Each Party shall, within five (5) business days after the Agreement Date, file with the FDA a notice letter, substantially in the form attached as Schedule XI(A) or Schedule XI(B) (as applicable), regarding EKR's license to use the Transferred NDA. PPI represents, warrants and covenants that: (i) prior to the Agreement Date, it has provided EKR with complete, up- to-date copies of the Transferred NDA and all material correspondence with Regulatory Authorities in the -15- Territory in connection with the Transferred NDA (including, but not limited to, any periodic and annual report submissions, and all adverse event reports and data) and (ii) on the Agreement Date, EKR shall receive the non-exclusive right to use the Transferred NDA, free and clear of any liens and encumbrances. For the avoidance of doubt, nothing in this Agreement regarding the appointment of EKR as PPI's distributor of the Products shall be construed to diminish any rights of EKR as licensee of the Transferred NDA. Upon termination of this Agreement for any reason, the license to use the Transferred NDA and related regulatory documentation granted to EKR shall terminate.
perturbation.explanation: The change from a transfer of 'all right, title and interest' to a 'perpetual, non-exclusive license' creates a significant in-text contradiction. Section 4.1 states that 'EKR shall own the Marketing Authorizations'. This is directly conflicted by section 3.2 (as modified) which indicates that EKR only has a license to the Transferred NDA and will have to terminate any use of NDA after termination. This directly impacts the value of the agreement for EKR and creates uncertainty about EKR's rights to commercialize the Product following the Agreement Date.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Marketing Authorizations. EKR shall, as determined in its sole discretion to be commercially reasonable, prepare studies of the markets and sales potential of the Products for countries in the Territory other than the United States and present such studies to the Committee. EKR shall at its own cost and expense use commercially reasonable efforts to take those steps reasonably necessary in order to obtain and thereafter maintain Marketing Authorizations (including pricing and reimbursement approvals) for the Product in those countries of the Territory other than the United States which the Committee determines to present commercially viable opportunities for the Product. EKR shall provide PPI with a copy of any original certificates of approval/registration in each country in the Territory other than the United States. EKR shall provide PPI with a copy of any other registration matters received from the appropriate Regulatory Authorities concerning maintenance, renewal or variations to the original certificates of approval/registration in each country in the Territory. Except as provided in Section 3.17, EKR shall be solely responsible for, and shall bear all costs associated with, all regulatory activities related to the development and approval of the Product in the countries of the Territory (including, after the Agreement Date, the United States) and shall own the Marketing Authorizations for the Product in each other country of the Territory. EKR will comply with all conditions and requirements attaching to such Marketing Authorizations.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that PPI 'sells, transfers, conveys and assigns to EKR all right, title and interest' in the Transferred NDA. The changed text modifies this to grant EKR a 'perpetual, non-exclusive license'. This is a direct contradiction because 'ownership' (original text) is fundamentally different from 'license' (changed text). The contradicted text, section 4.1, states 'EKR shall own the Marketing Authorizations'. If EKR only has a license as per the changed text, it cannot fully own the Marketing Authorizations, because a license grants permission to use but not ownership of the underlying intellectual property. The modified text therefore creates a clear inconsistency with section 4.1, making the agreement uncertain and potentially unenforceable regarding EKR's rights.

perturbation.original_text: 6.6 Payment. EKR shall pay to PPI, any Minimum Royalties and Additional Royalties due within forty-five (45) days of the end of each Quarter as the case may be subject to reconciliation at the end of each Calendar Year as set forth in Section 6.9.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.6 Payment. EKR shall pay to PPI, any Minimum Royalties and Additional Royalties due within ninety (90) days of the end of each Quarter as the case may be subject to reconciliation at the end of each Calendar Year as set forth in Section 6.9.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction related to payment timelines for royalties. Section 6.6 now states royalties are due within 90 days. However, Section 7.6 contains the original payment terms: 'Should any amount not be paid by either Party on or before the due date for payment interest on such unpaid amount at the rate of [**]% above the prime lending rate of Citibank, N.A. (or its successor in interest) in effect from time to time'. As the payment timeline is not clearly distinguished across clauses, creates ambiguity regarding when late payment interest applies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.6 Interest. Should any amount not be paid by either Party on or before the due date for payment interest on such unpaid amount at the rate of [**]% above the prime lending rate of Citibank, N.A. (or its successor in interest) in effect from time to time and such interest shall be calculated and payable in respect of the period from the date such amount is due until the date payment in full is received in cleared funds.
perturbation.justification: The change in Section 6.6 from 45 days to 90 days for royalty payments directly contradicts the payment timeline implied in Section 7.6, which refers to 'the due date for payment' and imposes interest on late payments. The altered due date in 6.6 now conflicts with the original understanding of 'due date' in 7.6, creating an inconsistency regarding when interest penalties apply, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
